summary_es: Este modulo expone las falacias fundamentales de NHST (Null Hypothesis Significance Testing) y su impacto en las finanzas. Se demuestra la falacia inversa: confundir P(datos|H0) con P(H0|datos), el error central de los p-values. Se aplica la falacia del fiscal al backtesting financiero, mostrando que con 200 estrategias nulas, se esperan ~10 falsos positivos con p<0.05. Se demuestra p-hacking con grados de libertad del investigador. Se explican los 3 errores de interpretacion de intervalos de confianza. Se construye un modelo OLS sintetico y se ejecutan tests diagnosticos (Jarque-Bera, Durbin-Watson) que demuestran que los supuestos de OLS se violan con datos financieros, invalidando los p-values. Se presenta la alternativa bayesiana como solucion.
summary_en: This module exposes the fundamental fallacies of NHST (Null Hypothesis Significance Testing) and their impact on finance. The inverse fallacy is demonstrated: confusing P(data|H0) with P(H0|data), the central error of p-values. The prosecutor's fallacy is applied to financial backtesting, showing that with 200 null strategies, ~10 false positives with p<0.05 are expected. P-hacking with researcher degrees of freedom is demonstrated. Three confidence interval interpretation errors are explained. A synthetic OLS model is built and diagnostic tests (Jarque-Bera, Durbin-Watson) show that OLS assumptions are violated with financial data, invalidating p-values. The Bayesian alternative is presented as the solution.
learning_objectives_es: Explicar la falacia inversa que subyace en NHST y p-values
Demostrar la falacia del fiscal en backtesting con multiples estrategias
Identificar p-hacking y los grados de libertad del investigador
Interpretar correctamente intervalos de confianza y sus 3 errores
Ejecutar tests diagnosticos de OLS y entender cuando los p-values mienten
learning_objectives_en: Explain the inverse fallacy underlying NHST and p-values
Demonstrate the prosecutor's fallacy in backtesting with multiple strategies
Identify p-hacking and researcher degrees of freedom
Correctly interpret confidence intervals and their 3 errors
Run OLS diagnostic tests and understand when p-values lie

parameters.name: NHST Fallacies
parameters.origin_phrase: P-values confuse P(data|H0) with P(H0|data) -- the inverse fallacy

parameters.name: Model Assumption Violations
parameters.origin_phrase: OLS p-values are invalid when residuals violate normality assumptions

parameters.name: False Discovery
parameters.origin_phrase: Most published NHST findings are false due to multiple testing and low base rates

parameters.name: Bayesian Alternative
parameters.origin_phrase: Bayesian inference answers the right question: P(H|data) not P(data|H)
